outcome_contract.why: SEO tech audit identified crawl waste (24 dead URLs in sitemap), content quality degradation (raw i18n keys visible to Google on transport guide pages), and sparse freshness signals (77% of sitemap without lastmod). These directly impact search indexing quality for a 1,246-URL multilingual travel site.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: All sitemap URLs return 200, no i18n keys leak in SSR HTML for how-to-get-here pages, /assistance redirects correctly, lastmod coverage ≥60%

engineering_coverage.applicable: Required: extend sitemap test (no `/directions/*`), verify SSR audit covers how-to-get-here PlanChoice/TransportNotice
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Add test assertions for each finding

engineering_coverage.applicable: Required: sitemap XML contract unchanged; guide content JSON `lastUpdated` field backfill
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Plan lastmod backfill script carefully to avoid bulk-today guard

engineering_coverage.applicable: Required: all independently deployable, reversible via revert
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Standard deploy; can ship fixes 1-3 first, lastmod backfill separately

stage_payload.decision_summary: Four open SEO findings need remediation on hostel-positano.com. The decision is how to structure the fixes: as a single bundled change or independent tasks, and what specific approach to use for each finding (particularly the i18n SSR fix and the lastmod backfill, which have options).
goals: Remove 24 dead `/directions/*` 404 URLs from the sitemap
Fix i18n key leakage on how-to-get-here guide pages (SSR rendering shows raw keys)
non_goals: Keyword research, content strategy, or SERP analysis (lp-seo Phases 1-3)
constraints: Static export build (`OUTPUT_EXPORT=1`) — no middleware, `_redirects` is the redirect mechanism
`assertNoBulkTodayLastmod()` guard rejects ≥95% same-day entries in batches of ≥50
assumptions: The i18n leakage is a namespace preload ordering issue, not a missing translation
`lastmod` backfill can be done via content JSON without schema changes

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Fixes must resolve the exact issues verified on the live site
Fixes should be independently deployable (no coupling between findings)
Each fix needs verifiable acceptance criteria in CI
Changes should not affect working pages or existing SEO signals
All findings are straightforward; avoid over-engineering

options_considered.Upside: Clean fix, removes the source. One-line change.
options_considered.Downside: None — canonical URLs already in sitemap via `listLocalizedPublicUrls()`

options_considered.Description: Add `/directions/` pattern to the filter function
options_considered.Downside: Less clean — generates paths then filters them out. Two places to maintain.

options_considered.Option: A: Ensure namespace preload in page server component
options_considered.Description: Verify `loadGuideI18nBundle()` completes before component render; add explicit preload if needed
options_considered.Downside: Needs careful investigation of RSC execution order
options_considered.Key risks: May need to restructure component tree

options_considered.Option: B: Move PlanChoice/TransportNotice to client-only rendering
options_considered.Description: Wrap in `<ClientOnly>` or equivalent — don't attempt SSR for these
options_considered.Downside: Hides content from crawlers entirely — defeats the SEO purpose
options_considered.Key risks: Worsens SEO rather than fixing it

options_considered.Option: A: Backfill `lastUpdated` in guide content JSON using git dates
options_considered.Description: Build-time script extracts git commit dates for each content file, writes `lastUpdated` field
options_considered.Upside: Accurate per-file dates. Survives build. Google gets meaningful freshness signals.
options_considered.Downside: Needs git history in CI (shallow clones may lack it). Script to write across 18 locales × ~128 guides.
options_considered.Key risks: CI shallow clone gives same date for all files

options_considered.Option: B: Use build timestamp as fallback
options_considered.Description: For any sitemap entry without `lastmod`, use the build timestamp
options_considered.Downside: Misleading — tells Google every page was updated today. Bulk-today guard would reject it.
options_considered.Key risks: Actively harmful to SEO freshness signal

options_considered.Description: Manually add `lastUpdated` to content JSON files with known dates
options_considered.Upside: Most accurate for files with known dates
options_considered.Downside: Doesn't scale; doesn't help for files with unknown dates

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Execute all 4 fixes as independent tasks within a single plan. Ship findings #1 (sitemap), #3 (redirect), and #2 (i18n) together as they're small, well-understood changes. Ship finding #4 (lastmod backfill) as a separate task since it involves a scripted bulk content update.
chosen_approach.Why this wins: Each fix is well-bounded, independently deployable, and has clear acceptance criteria. Bundling #1 + #2 + #3 minimizes deployment overhead while keeping #4 separate avoids risking the straightforward fixes on the more complex lastmod automation.

rejected_approaches: **Client-only rendering for PlanChoice/TransportNotice** — defeats SEO purpose; hiding content from crawlers is the opposite of the goal
**Build timestamp as lastmod fallback** — misleading signal, blocked by bulk-today guard
**Filter-based sitemap exclusion** — works but inferior to removing the source

sequencing_constraints: Tasks 1, 2, 3 are fully independent — can execute in parallel
Task 4 is independent but should ship separately (bulk content change)
risks_to_carry_forward: i18n fix may be more complex than a preload ordering change if RSC boundary prevents namespace sharing
lastmod git-date approach needs CI depth check
